creator: nimaje+fbz@bureaucracy.de
text: so, that isn't a problem with openjdk, the problem is that openjfx just isn't portable and it seems that xpipe depends on stuff introduced with openjfx 22, so someone would need to port that and sadly that isn't as easy as copying the openjfx14 port change some version numbers and regenerate patches (I tried that with openjfx21 and it build but then segfaulted at runtime) (and while not meant to be used like that you can use openjfx14 with openjdk21)
